title: Status application profile
principles: Status application depends on the status probability of each eligible damage instance and the frequency at which eligible instances occur.
Status probability, opportunities per attack event, and opportunity frequency are distinct properties and must not be treated as interchangeable.
Status chance and opportunity signals must refer to the same attack mode, damage component, and conditional state.
Only instances explicitly eligible to produce status effects may contribute to the status opportunity profile.
Mechanical status opportunities must remain separate from contact-adjusted opportunities unless valid-contact evidence is explicitly supplied.
Qualitative status classifications require an explicit comparison context and must not rely on universal thresholds.
A favorable status application profile does not establish the usefulness, type distribution, stack behavior, or primary function of the resulting status effects.

aligned: Status probability and eligible opportunity frequency are both favorable within the supplied comparison context.
The evaluated scope supports recurring status application.
frequency_weighted: The evaluated scope produces frequent eligible opportunities with comparatively limited status probability per instance.
Recurring application depends strongly on the number of valid opportunities.
probability_weighted: The evaluated scope has favorable status probability but comparatively limited eligible opportunity frequency.
Applications may be reliable per valid instance without occurring frequently over time.
limited: Neither status probability nor eligible opportunity frequency is favorable within the supplied comparison context.
The supplied relationship provides limited evidence for recurring status application.
undetermined: Status probability, opportunity eligibility, opportunity frequency, scope alignment, or comparison context is insufficiently defined.
The status application relationship cannot be classified confidently.

status_chance_at_or_above_100_percent: Each complete 100 percent guarantees one status effect from an eligible damage instance.
The remaining percentage determines the chance of one additional status effect.
Do not infer which status types are selected without explicit damage-weighting or status-distribution evidence.
multiple_eligible_instances: Use the normalized eligible-instance profile supplied by multi-instance analysis.
Do not recount pellets, multishot, ticks, or components independently within this concept.
contact_validity_supplied: Adjust mechanical opportunities only with explicit contact-validity evidence associated with the same mode and component.
Do not infer practical contact from accuracy, spread, projectile, or beam labels alone.
multiple_damage_components: Preserve separate status probabilities, eligibility rules, and opportunity frequencies for every component.
Do not combine them into one aggregate profile unless the aggregation method and component relationships are explicitly defined.
conditional_status_profile: Preserve the activation condition and evaluate conditional status values separately from the base profile.
Do not describe conditional probability or eligibility as permanently active.

exceptions: Do not infer status opportunity frequency from status chance alone.
Do not assume every projectile, pellet, beam event, chained event, or damage component is independently status-eligible.
Do not multiply status chance by fire rate, multishot, pellet count, or component count without validated scope and eligibility relationships.
Do not infer beam tick frequency from fire rate or beam classification.
Do not infer status type distribution, proc utility, stack behavior, or primary job from the status application profile alone.
